localizedDescriptions.en: Document to communicate information to others, commonly in response to a request from another party.

children.name: Ehic Carrier Id

localizedDescriptions.en: A unique personal identifier.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.person_identifiable_parent.v1 and name/value='Patient']/items[at0001 and name/value='Ehic Carrier Id']/value

localizedDescriptions.en: A unique personal identifier.
A unique personal identifier.
Personal name compliant with European ISA standard.
A family name is usually shared by members of a family. This attribute also carries prefixes or suffixes which are part of the Family Name, e.g. “de Boer”, “van de Putte”, “von und zu Orlow”.

annotations.comment: Multiple family names, such as are commonly found in Hispanic countries, are recorded in the single Family Name field so that, for example, Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra's Family Name would be recorded as "de Cervantes Saavedra.

localizedDescriptions.en: A given name, or multiple given names, are the denominator(s) that identify an individual within a family. These are given to a person by his or her parents at birth or may be legally recognised as 'given names' through a formal process.

annotations.comment: All given names are ordered in one field so that, for example, the Given Name for Johan Sebastian Bach is 'Johan Sebastian.'

localizedDescriptions.en: The person's date of birth.
Anonymised details of a person.
Ther current administrative gender of the person.
The sex / gender is male.
The sex / gnder is female.
The sex/ gender is indeterminate.
Details of person's death aligned with PARENT Common Data Elements for registry use.
The date of the subject's death.
Details of a healthcare professional.
Personal name compliant with European ISA standard.

The full name (fullName) property contains the complete name of a person as one string. In addition to the content of given name, family name and, in some systems, patronymic name, this can carry additional parts of a person’s name such as titles, middle names or suffixes like “the third” or names which are neither a given nor a family name.
The healthcare worker's profesional identifier.
Details of a healthcare provider organisation.
The name of the organisation.
The unique identifier of the organisation.
Address details compliant with European ISA Standard.
An address component that represents the name of a passage or way through from one location to another. A thoroughfare is not necessarily a road, it might be a waterway or some other feature.

children.name: Locator Designator (street number)

localizedDescriptions.en: this is the building number, apartment number, etc.

children.aqlPath: /context/other_context[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.healthcare_professional_parent.v1 and name/value='Reporting professional']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.healthcare_provider_parent.v1]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address_isa.v1]/items[at0004 and name/value='Locator Designator (street number)']/value

annotations.comment: For an address such as "Flat 3, 17 Bridge Street", the locator is "flat 3, 17."

localizedDescriptions.en: The key postal division of the address, usually the city.
The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.
The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country.
The name of a specific department within the organisation.
Contact details for the heathcare worker.

annotations.comment: For example: "No known operations or significant procedures" or "No previous" (appendicectomy).

children.name: Consent to share information

localizedDescriptions.en: Record of status and details of informed consent from a patient (or patient's agent) for a proposed procedure, trial or other healthcare-related activity (including treatments and investigations), based upon a clear appreciation and understanding of the facts, implications, and possible future consequences by the patient or patient's agent.

children.name: Procedure/Trial/Activity

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the procedure, clinical trial or healthcare-related activity (including correct side/correct site, where appropriate) against which the consent status and details are recorded.

localizedDescriptions.en: Need for informed consent is identified.
Intent to request informed consent has been postponed.
Intent to request informed consent has been cancelled, prior to requesting consent from patient or patient's agent..
An appointment has been scheduled to request consent.
Informed consent has been requested from the patient or patient's agent, but no response has been received.
Informed consent has been provided by the patient or patient's agent.
Informed consent was not obtainable from either the patient or patient's advocate.
In response to a request for informed consent, it has been refused by patient or patient's agent.
Following initial provision of informed consent, it has been withrawn by the patient or patient's agent.
Informed consent has been provided by the patient or patient's agent and the activity is now complete.

localizedDescriptions.en: Height, or body length, is measured from crown of head to sole of foot.  Height is measured with the individual in a standing position and body length in a recumbent position.

Any event.

Less than 70.

children.name: Hip device components

localizedDescriptions.en: An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']

children.name: Device name

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']/items[at0001]/value

annotations.comment:
This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. With specific examples.

 Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

annotations.comment:
This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. With specific examples.

 Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

children.name: Type

localizedDescriptions.en: The category or kind of device.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Acetabular component']/items[at0003]/value

annotations.comment:
Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Acetabular ring component']/items[at0003]/value

annotations.comment:
Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.

children.name: Head component

localizedDescriptions.en: An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Head component']

children.name: Manufacturer device name

localizedDescriptions.en: Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/description[at0001]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Hip device components']/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1 and name/value='Head component']/items[at0001 and name/value='Manufacturer device name']/value

annotations.comment:
This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. With specific examples.

 Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.

localizedDescriptions.en: Additional hip procedure details to align with Valdoltra hospital hip registry.
Was trochanter osteotomy performed?
Trochanter osteotomy was performed.
Trochanter osteotomy was not performed.
Were prophylactic systemic antibiotics used?
Systemic antibiotic prophylaxis was given to the patient.
Systemic antibiotic prophylaxis was not given to the patient.
Was wound drainage used?
Wound drainage was used.
Wound drainage was not used.
The duration of the hip procedure.
Record details about an anatomical location.
Specific and identified anatomical location.
Named anatomical location - ideally would be coded where possible.
Specify lateraility of the anatomical location.
Left laterality.
Right laterality.
Details about any complication arising from the procedure.
The ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service. This is also referred to as Filler Order Identifier.
Details of a healthcare provider organisation.
The name of the organisation.
The unique identifier of the organisation.
Address details compliant with European ISA Standard.
An address component that represents the name of a passage or way through from one location to another. A thoroughfare is not necessarily a road, it might be a waterway or some other feature.

children.name: Locator Designator (street number0

localizedDescriptions.en: this is the building number, apartment number, etc.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-SECTION.adhoc.v1 and name/value='Current procedure']/items[openEHR-EHR-ACTION.procedure-primary_hip_arthroplasty_res.v1]/protocol[at0053]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.healthcare_provider_parent.v1]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.address_isa.v1]/items[at0004 and name/value='Locator Designator (street number0']/value

annotations.comment: For an address such as "Flat 3, 17 Bridge Street", the locator is "flat 3, 17."

localizedDescriptions.en: The key postal division of the address, usually the city.
The region of the address, usually a county, state or other such area that typically encompasses several localities.
The uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country.
The name of a specific department within the organisation.
Contact details for the organisation.
Request for procedure is initiated.
Request for procedure sent.
Request for procedure is postponed.
Procedure request has been cancelled.
Procedure has been scheduled.
Procedure is being carried out.
Procedure has been suspended.
Procedure has been aborted.
Procedure has been completed.
Procedure report has been written.
Procedure report has been attested.
Procedure report has been distributed.
